ref: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
text: A Yesu na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ kpɛ̃n a gboro chen jiyuo 'lɔ wʊ yõ. Tɩchɛ a nɩyɔɔ na wa wõ a nɩtɔ̃ ba yin a tẽn ala pɔ bɩɛr wʊ nɩ gbɛɛ chen a be.
translated: Jesus and his mother were there, he was invited to a big house to eat with them. The crowd and his disciples were there, and there was not enough beer for them to drink.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ nyɛrɛ na 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ jie jaa, tɩchɛ a wʊ mãɛ tẽe nɩ a wʊ yir dẽme nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ sɛ̃ lɛ waa nyɛrɛ 'yɔ̃ɔfʊ ɛ.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few, pray therefore the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest."

ref: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
text: 'Lɔ lɛ a bʊ̃bie a jaa pɔ bʊ̃bile tɩchɛ 'lɔ wa nʊ̃ɔ, 'lɔ lɛ ma ɩ a tɩɩr a jaa pɔ tɩkpɛ̃ɛ, ka lubil ma 'yɔ̃ɔ choor a wʊ ju.”
translated: They said that the rabbit and the elephant were friends, they said that they would go and take a walk together, and the rabbit's wife prepared food for them to eat.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: A yɩn Kapenahum dẽme, ban 'mɔɔ yɩn don saaju bɩɩ? Kai, ban suun yɩn a kpĩisɩ tẽe. Ala ɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃ a yɩ pɔ mɩ ta tʊ̃n a Sodom pɔ, a tẽe anyãna naa chɛnɛ na wa tɔ dɩna.
translated: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted to the heavens? No, you will go down to Hades. For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Be lɛ ka Hɛrɔd sɔɔl bʊɔl a yɛ̃ dẽme bala sʊʊr ba a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a ŋmɛrbir na pur wa.
translated: It was then that Herod's anger was strong and he ordered the killing of all the male children in Bethlehem town.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu na bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛrɛ, a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Tẽe 'lɔ na ŋma dẽdẽme jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ kor ɛ. Tẽe 'lɔ bɩɩ yir kpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ na ŋma dẽdẽme jɛɛrɛ taa kʊ ara ɛ.
translated: Jesus and his disciples were going along the road, he said to them, "Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant. Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all."

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Wʊn wa tɩ piel guoro a libie yele ba nyɔɔn ãsʊɔ na di wʊ turo pie sãn waan a wʊ sɛ̃,
translated: They said that the child's mother was a bad person and that is why the child does not hear and does not speak.

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Be lɛ ba pãa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a ba ɩ a paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl lɛ ɛ tɩchɛ ba bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne wiilfʊ.
translated: They were the Pharisees and the Sadducees who were questioning him.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ban wa kpa wʊ a daa ju baar, ba põ naa a wʊ fɔɔr ŋmen gbãm bɔ̃ɔn ala ãsʊɔ jaa nɩ na di,
translated: They said that the people who were in the bush, they were afraid that the people would come and take their farms and burn their houses and take their things.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na turo a Nãaŋmɩn sɔr, 'lɔ lɛ a n yɛbɛ nɩ a n yɛpɔɔ nɩ a n ma.”
translated: The people said, "We will go to the Naangmin sorghum, we will eat and drink and dance there."

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: “Wʊ lɛ tʊ̃n nɩyor ɩka ba tɩ 'yɛr a bala wʊn bʊɔl, ‘Nyɛ, n maal a pɔɔ paalaa bʊ̃diir baar, n kʊn a n nadar nɩ a napɔl ka yele jaa chɔɔr baar, yɩ wa a pɔɔ paalaa jie.’
translated: “We went to the bush and found a small antelope, ‘Well, I shot it with my arrow and killed it, I carried it on my head and my back and brought it home to my wife to cook it, so that we can eat it together.’”

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu wa sɩɩr ba tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ ir, yɩ ta joro dãbãɛ ɛ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to the disciples, "You go, you prepare a lot of food."

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: na ba gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a puo sʊɔ 'yɛrɛ, ‘A nɩbɛ banyãna wa bɩɛr wa tɩ fʊ yab sɩ jãtaa, nɩ sɩn na ŋmaa ir nɩ ɔɔr wa tʊ̃ lɛ̃ɛɛ ka mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ ŋme sɩ tɩ mʊrʊ̃.’
translated: They said to the rabbit, 'We are coming to your house to dance, and you must prepare a lot of food for us to eat, and you must also make a big fire so that we can warm ourselves.'

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Tɩchɛ ka a pɔɔ wa gbur dumo a wʊ niem 'yɛr, “N Soro, sʊ̃ʊ ma.”
translated: The rabbit said to his wife in the evening, "My Soro, let's go."

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ ɔ̃ɔ ɩ ɔ̃ɔ ka a yɩ kai ɩ kai, bon 'lɔ na gar a nɩtɔ̃ yin a dɛɛr sʊɔ sɛ̃.
translated: They said that they would give us food, but they didn't give us food, so we went to the bush to look for something to eat.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Ka a nɛɛr 'maa a nɩbɛ ka ba lɩɛb sʊrɔ taa, “Bo lɛ? Wiil paal bo mɩ nɩ nyã, na tara kpɛ̃ɔ lɛ! Wʊ ma 'yɛr 'yɛr, ka a sɩdɛbɛ ma tu a wʊ nɛɛ.”
translated: The woman said to her husband, "What's wrong? You have not given me food to eat, and the children are crying! You are just sitting there, and your wife is suffering because of you."

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: Wʊn wa tɩ ara a dɔ̃dɔr nɛɛ, a pɔɔbile ãsʊɔ nyɛ wʊ naa na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ na ara a be, “A daba nyã mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Najaretɩ.”
translated: One day, an angel came from God, an angel appeared to a certain woman and the woman was startled and the angel said, "You will give birth to a boy and his name will be Jesus of Nazareth."

ref: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
text: Fʊn taa den a kãnyũu anyãna da libiyɔɔ de kʊ a nɔ̃n dẽme.”
translated: The man said that he would go to his farm and then come back home to eat.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Ka ba 'mataa kul a wʊ yir tɩ jɛ̃ dire ka a lõpodiere nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ yɔɔ wa pʊɔ ba nɩ a Yesu poturbo ka ba 'mataa dire.
translated: They said that the people of the village would not allow them to enter the village, and they were standing outside the village, when Jesus appeared and they said that the people would allow them to enter.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka bala jaa pɔɔ na dɔɔ ka a wɛr pɔ, ba ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ gar a Jɔɔn kɔ̃suor ɛ, tɩchɛ 'lɔ mɩ na ɩ a pʊʊr a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ gar wʊ naa.
translated: The rabbit and the squirrel went to the forest to look for food, but they didn't find any, so they went to John's farm, where they saw that he had a lot of millet in his granary.
